title: blepharipappus heterotrichus
intro: Blepharipappus heterotrichus, also known as Wyoming fleabane, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the western United States and Canada. The medicinal properties of Blepharipappus heterotrichus are primarily attributed to its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Blepharipappus heterotrichus include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes.
